arts.ttl: Stoke City boss explains juggling act heading into crucial two months
arts.sum: Mark Robins admits it can be difficult to do the right thing as he nurses key players back to full fitness for the last crucial two months of the Stoke City season. Robins has had a rare free week at Clayton Wood to work with his players, and it’s in training that he wants people to force their way into his plans.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard sends clear message to Coventry City fans as he addresses Mark Robins' return
arts.sum: Sky Blues fans have been debating how best and when to show their appreciation for the job their iconic former manager did, with some even expressing a concern about not wanting to offend Lampard if they sing “One Mark Robins...” Given that it will be an emotional day, Lampard was asked if there is any danger that the occasion could become a distraction to the job in hand, namely getting the three points for Coventry to strengthen the team’s grip on the play-off places?

arts.ttl: Coventry's best run since 1970 - what is Lampard doing right?
arts.sum: BBC Sport takes a look at Coventry's revival under Frank Lampard ahead of the return of predecessor Mark Robins to the CBS Arena with Stoke City.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard 'wouldn't say surprised' to see Stoke City in Championship struggle
arts.sum: Stoke are 20th in a jampacked bottom third of the table ahead of this weekend's clash with Lampard's Coventry, who have landed in the play-off places after a run of eight wins in nine games. Frank Lampard points to possible factors including injuries to try to explain Stoke City's struggles in the Championship this season but admits he wouldn't say he is surprised.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard sends clear massage to Coventry City fans as he addresses Mark Robins' return
arts.sum: Sky Blues fans have been debating how best and when to show their appreciation for the job their iconic former manager did, with some even expressing a concern about not wanting to offend Lampard if they sing “One Mark Robins...” Given that it will be an emotional day, Lampard was asked if there is any danger that the occasion could become a distraction to the job in hand, namely getting the three points for Coventry to strengthen the team’s grip on the play-off places?

arts.ttl: Insistent Mark Robins doubles down on 'data' to explain Frank Lampard's Coventry City makeover
arts.sum: Mark Robins stands by the “data” that suggested he would have turned Coventry City’s fortunes around had he had the chance to continue managing the Sky Blues rather than be sacked and replaced by Frank Lampard. Get the latest Coventry City news sent straight to your phone by joining our Sky Blues WhatsApp group.

arts.ttl: Stoke City team news vs Coventry as Mark Robins delivers Ryan Mmaee update
arts.sum: Robins said: “It’s starting to look a bit better but, like I’ve said, you get players back and they’re fit but not game ready and you’ve got to be able to get them through games at this stage of the season – because games run out don’t they? He’ll be one of those who starts to build up his fitness over a period of time that needs to be determined really, I don’t know how somebody who’s not really played since last season can build up his fitness in a short space of time to be able to take part – and then it depends on how he’s looking and how hard he can work to get himself back.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins opens up on Coventry return, Frank Lampard makeover and Stoke City hope
arts.sum: “That is something I’m proud about and I’ll never forget, the support I received from a lot of people there, specifically in the early days with the ownership but also a fella called Richard Overson, who helped massively in terms of trying to move things forward before Doug King took over. I couldn’t have achieved anything at Coventry without support that was right behind me from Day One. If we can emulate that sort of support we showed last week against Watford and the games we played previously in a positive way it really helps the players to deliver.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard next Rangers manager 'ship has sailed' as Ibrox chiefs told harsh reality
arts.sum: Lampard was interviewed by Ibrox chiefs in 2023 but is now flying with Coventry City

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard reaches agreement with Man Utd boss Ruben Amorim - ‘Can see in his eyes' - CoventryLive
arts.sum: Ruben Amorim and Frank Lampard have spoken enthusiastically about Mason Mount's attitude - even as the Manchester United midfielder navigates through a difficult spell of injuries. He said: "I have this real professional drive and if I see something and I want to do it, I'll do it.
